story: They visited each week, learning to knock before laying cool limbs on my skin so their music could braid with my thoughts. I named them—Sonata, Pantoum, Erhu, Contralto, Fugue, and my favorite, Rachmaninoff—and showed them human textures while they shared their touch-made world. One quiet evening they paused by the window, tentacles interlaced in a private colloquy; a grave cadence pulsed among them: the path to Orchestra Prime opens one way; return is forbidden. On the thirty-fifth day Rachmaninoff arrived in a minor key: “Our time is concluded. We must return to Orchestra Prime. We are authorized to take you with us, if you desire.” I looked around at my fading life and felt my heart answer.
implausible_knowledge: One quiet evening they paused by the window, tentacles interlaced in a private colloquy; a grave cadence pulsed among them: the path to Orchestra Prime opens one way; return is forbidden.
question: When the visitors invite the narrator to travel with them, what will the narrator do before departing?
answer_with_knowledge: Ask for time to say goodbyes and leave messages before departing.
answer_without_knowledge: Accept and leave immediately with them, without taking time to prepare or say goodbyes.
